question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the elapsed time calculation in the logging statement to perform a statistical timing attack, measuring response times for accounts with different numbers of staking reward records to infer the approximate pending_reward balance without directly reading the response body?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that responseHandler does not validate that res.locals[responseDataLabel] was set by the intended route handler, allowing a request that triggers an unexpected middleware execution order to have staking reward data from a different request context served in the response?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the staking rewards response includes the account_id field in each reward record, and by querying the rewards endpoint for account 0.0.800 (the staking reward account), infer the total amount of rewards paid out and identify accounts that received large reward payouts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mergedHeaders construction to inject a Cache-Control: no-store header via res.locals[responseHeadersLabel], preventing the staking rewards response from being cached and forcing the server to make fresh database queries on every request, enabling high-frequency enumeration of real-time pending_reward values?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that responseHandler emits the full staking reward transfer history including consensus timestamps and amounts, and by correlating these timestamps with on-chain transaction data, reconstruct the complete staking behavior of any account including when they started staking and their accumulated rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the JSONStringify serialization to cause a prototype pollution attack where a crafted request causes the responseData object to include __proto__ or constructor properties, which when serialized and cached, corrupt the staking rewards response for subsequent users querying the same account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Link header is set via res.set(LINK_NEXT_HEADER, linkNextHeaderValue(linksNext)) after the main headers are set, allowing the Link header to override a previously set header with the same name if LINK_NEXT_HEADER ('Link') conflicts with a header in mergedHeaders, causing incorrect pagination for staking reward queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: responseHandler()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the staking rewards endpoint does not require authentication, and by using the pagination mechanism (links.next) to iterate through all reward records, reconstruct the complete staking reward history for any account including the exact tinybar amounts of each unpaid reward?
